senses.definition: to go out and drink alcohol, dance, or do whatever one pleases

examples.x: To celebrate their anniversary, they decided to paint the town and enjoy a night on the town.
After winning the championship, the team decided to paint the town red with a victory parade and celebrations.
They hired a limousine and invited friends to join them in painting the town red for her birthday.
Graduation day was the perfect occasion to paint the town, with parties, dancing, and laughter lasting late into the night.
For New Year's Eve, the city came alive with fireworks and festivities as people gathered to paint the town in celebration.

senses.definition: to celebrate the end of the year and prepare for a new year

examples.x: As midnight approaches, Buddhist temples around the country begin ringing out the old year.
Ringing out the old and ringing in the new at the turn of the year invariably involves wishful thinking.
They held a ceremony to ring out the old traditions and embrace new ones in their cultural celebration.
With the start of the new academic year, the school administration decided to ring out the old curriculum and introduce a more modern approach to teaching.

senses.definition: to play the piano, typically in a skillful or enjoyable manner

examples.x: My grandfather loves playing the piano; he tickles the ivories whenever he can.
At the jazz club, the pianist took the stage and began to tickle the ivories, captivating the audience with his improvisation.
As a child, he showed a natural talent for music and could tickle the ivories like a seasoned pianist.
She loves to sit at the grand piano and tickle the ivories, creating beautiful melodies.
After a long day at work, she finds solace in her piano, spending hours tickling the ivories to unwind and relax.

senses.definition: (of music, shows, etc.) begin broadcasting, typically through radio or television

examples.x: The breaking news story hit the airwaves, reaching millions of listeners within minutes.
"Their new album will hit the airwaves next week, and fans are eagerly awaiting its release.
The radio talk show host is known for her witty commentary that hits the airwaves every morning.
Political candidates often use radio ads to hit the airwaves and promote their campaigns.
The company's advertising campaign will hit the airwaves during prime time, targeting a broad audience.

senses.definition: said by children during Halloween to request candy or other treats from the homeowner

examples.x: On Halloween night, the kids dressed as ghosts and witches went from house to house, shouting 'Trick or treat!'
The Halloween party was filled with children excited to play 'trick or treat' and receive sweet goodies.
The neighbors prepared for the Halloween festivities by stocking up on candies to give to all the kids saying 'trick or treat.
The playful threat of 'trick or treat' delighted both children and adults as they enjoyed the Halloween tradition.
In the spirit of Halloween, the community organized a 'trick or treat' event, allowing children to visit local businesses for treats and fun.

senses.definition: to celebrate the birth of a baby, particularly by having an alcoholic drink

examples.x: Turns out that David Pickering and friends are wetting the baby's head.

senses.definition: to go out onto a dance floor and start dancing

examples.x: The younger people took the floor, and all the elders sneaked back to their chairs.
When the music started, she was the first to take the floor and showcase her dancing skills.
At the ballroom competition, the couple couldn't wait to take the floor and perform their elegant waltz routine.
In the crowded nightclub, he was eager to take the floor and dance to his favorite tunes.
During the salsa class, participants were encouraged to take the floor and practice their moves.

senses.definition: an event in which people, often women, can swap items of clothing or accessories with their friends or acquaintances

examples.x: She attended a swishing party to refresh her wardrobe, finding unique pieces that suited her style.
He participated in a swishing party and discovered a stunning vintage dress that became his new favorite outfit.
The annual swishing party became a tradition for friends who enjoyed updating their wardrobes in an eco-friendly way.

senses.definition: to have a really good time or enjoy oneself tremendously in a specific activity or event

examples.x: I hope you have a blast at the concert tonight; the band is fantastic!
The kids had a blast at the amusement park, riding all the thrilling rides.
We had a blast at the beach, enjoying the sun, surf, and building sandcastles.
The surprise party was a total success, and the guest of honor had a blast.
Despite the rainy weather, we had a blast during our board game night, laughing and competing all evening.

senses.definition: to dance, particularly in a nice way

examples.x: Did you see grandpa busting a move out there?
Get a load of her when she busts a move.
At the dance battle, he was the first to bust a move, impressing the crowd with his breakdancing skills.
Whenever the DJ plays their favorite song, they can't resist the urge to bust a move on the dance floor
In the park, a group of young people gathered to bust a move, showcasing their urban dance talents to passersby.
It's odd seeing pictures of my granddad tearing up the dance floor in his zoot suit as a young man.
At the disco club, she tore up the dance floor with her electrifying dance moves, setting the tone for an unforgettable night.
As soon as the music started, the wedding guests couldn't wait to tear up the dance floor and celebrate the joyous occasion.
During the talent show, the young hip-hop dancer tore up the dance floor with a routine that earned a standing ovation.

senses.definition: to enjoy oneself to a great extent

examples.x: Outside the boys were sitting on the ground and, judging by the gales of laughter, they were having a ball.
e had a ball at the beach, playing in the sun and building sandcastles all day.
The children had a ball at the amusement park, riding the roller coasters and eating cotton candy.
The annual masquerade ball is known for being an event where people truly have a ball, dancing and socializing in style.
The family reunion was a fantastic event, and all generations had a ball, reminiscing and creating new memories together.

senses.definition: an extremely enjoyable or thrilling time
